similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths describing Imam Ali's personal agricultural labor, including planting and cultivating date palms, and dedicating the proceeds as charitable endowments (waqf).
Narrations about the Imams modeling a simple, labor-based lifestyle and earning their sustenance through manual work, as an example for their followers.
Hadiths about the blessings (baraka) associated with combining tawakkul (reliance on God's will) with active effort and work, resulting in miraculous or extraordinary outcomes.

summaries.bn: এই হাদিসটি বর্ণনা করে যে আমিরুল মুমিনিন ইমাম আলী (আ.স.) খেজুরের বিচি বহন করে বের হতেন এবং সেগুলো রোপণ করতেন, আল্লাহর ইচ্ছার উপর নির্ভর করে। তাঁর রোপণ করা প্রতিটি বিচিই অঙ্কুরিত হতো। ইমাম আল-সাদিক (আ.স.) কর্তৃক বর্ণিত এই হাদিসটি জীবিকার ক্ষেত্রে ইমামদের অনুসরণ বিষয়ক অধ্যায়ে রয়েছে।
summaries.de: Dieser Hadith berichtet, dass Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (Friede sei mit ihm), der Anführer der Gläubigen, regelmäßig Dattelkerne mit sich trug, um sie zu pflanzen, und dabei sein Vertrauen auf den göttlichen Willen ausdrückte. Jeder Kern, den er pflanzte, keimte erfolgreich. Diese Überlieferung, die vom Imam al-Sadiq (Friede sei mit ihm) stammt, steht im Kapitel über die Nachahmung der Imame im Lebensunterhalt und betont die Verbindung von Arbeit und Tawakkul.
summaries.en: This hadith narrates that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS), the Commander of the Believers, would regularly go out carrying loads of date stones and plant them as palm trees, saying 'In sha' Allah' (if God wills) when asked about them. Remarkably, every single date stone he planted would successfully grow into a palm tree. Transmitted through Imam al-Sadiq (AS), this account appears in the chapter on emulating the Imams' example in livelihood, highlighting how the first Imam's combination of personal labor and reliance on God's will resulted in complete success — serving as a model for how believers should approach their work.
summaries.es: Este hadiz narra que el Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (la paz sea con él), el Comandante de los Creyentes, salía cargando huesos de dátil para plantarlos, expresando su confianza en la voluntad de Dios. Cada semilla que plantaba crecía exitosamente. Transmitida por el Imam al-Sadiq (la paz sea con él), esta narración aparece en el capítulo sobre emular a los Imames en el sustento, destacando la unión del trabajo manual y el tawakkul.
summaries.fa: این حدیث نقل می‌کند که امیرالمومنین علی بن ابی‌طالب علیه‌السلام هسته‌های خرما حمل می‌کرد و آنها را با توکل به اراده الهی می‌کاشت، و هر هسته‌ای که می‌کاشت به درخت تبدیل می‌شد. این روایت از امام صادق علیه‌السلام نقل شده و در باب اقتدا به ائمه در معاش قرار دارد، نشان می‌دهد که تلاش و توکل با هم برکت می‌آورند.
summaries.fr: Ce hadith rapporte que l'Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (que la paix soit sur lui), le Commandant des Croyants, sortait régulièrement avec des charges de noyaux de dattes pour les planter, en exprimant sa confiance en la volonté divine. Chaque noyau qu'il plantait germait avec succès. Transmise par l'Imam al-Sadiq (que la paix soit sur lui), cette narration figure dans le chapitre sur l'imitation des Imams en matière de subsistance, soulignant l'union du travail et du tawakkul.
summaries.id: Hadis ini menceritakan bahwa Imam Ali bin Abi Thalib (AS), Amirul Mukminin, biasa membawa biji-biji kurma untuk ditanam sambil mengucapkan 'insya Allah' sebagai ungkapan tawakal kepada kehendak Allah. Setiap biji yang beliau tanam tumbuh menjadi pohon kurma. Riwayat dari Imam al-Shadiq (AS) ini tercantum dalam bab tentang meneladani para Imam dalam urusan penghidupan, menekankan nilai kerja keras dan tawakkal yang menghasilkan keberkahan.
summaries.ru: Этот хадис рассказывает о том, что имам Али ибн Аби Талиб (мир ему), Повелитель верующих, регулярно выходил с финиковыми косточками, чтобы их посадить, выражая уповение на волю Аллаха. Каждая посаженная им косточка давала всходы. Переданный от имама ас-Садика (мир ему), этот хадис относится к главе о следовании примеру имамов в вопросах пропитания, подчёркивая соединение труда и таваккуля.
summaries.tr: Bu hadis, Mü'minlerin Emiri Hazret Ali'nin (a.s.) hurma çekirdekleri taşıyarak dışarı çıktığını ve bunları ektiğini, Allah'ın iradesiyle hiçbir tanesinin büyümeden kalmadığını aktarmaktadır. İmam Sadık (a.s.) tarafından nakledilen bu rivayet, İmamların geçim konusundaki örnekliğine uymayı anlatan bölümde yer almakta ve çalışma, tevekkül ve bereketin nasıl bir arada gerçekleştiğini vurgulamaktadır.
summaries.ur: یہ حدیث بیان کرتی ہے کہ امیر المومنین امام علی علیہ السلام کھجور کی گٹھلیاں لے کر باہر نکلتے اور انہیں لگاتے تھے، اور ہر گٹھلی سے درخت اگتا تھا۔ امام صادق علیہ السلام نے اس واقعے کو ان ابواب میں نقل کیا ہے جو ائمہ کی سیرت کو ذریعۂ معاش کے معاملے میں پیروی کرنے سے متعلق ہیں، یہ ظاہر کرتے ہوئے کہ محنت اور توکل علی اللہ کا امتزاج کتنا مبارک ہوتا ہے۔
summaries.zh: 这段圣训记述了众信士的长官伊玛目阿里（愿主赐福于他）经常携带枣核外出种植，并以「如果安拉意愿」表达对神圣意志的依托（tawakkul）。他所种下的每一颗枣核都成功发芽长成椰枣树。此圣训由伊玛目萨迪格（愿主赐福于他）传述，收录于关于效法伊玛目在谋生方面榜样的篇章，强调了劳动与托靠安拉相结合的美德。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/19)
text: إِنَّ أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) كَانَ يَخْرُجُ وَ مَعَهُ أَحْمَالُ النَّوَى فَيُقَالُ لَهُ يَا أَبَا الْحَسَنِ مَا هَذَا مَعَكَ فَيَقُولُ نَخْلٌ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ فَيَغْرِسُهُ فَلَمْ يُغَادَرْ مِنْهُ وَاحِدَةٌ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said that Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> went out and with him<sup>asws</sup> was a load of date stones. So it was said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘O Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>! What is this with you<sup>asws</sup>?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Palm trees, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Willing’. So he<sup>asws</sup> planted them, and not a single one of them failed to sprout’.
en.sarwar: 9. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ibn Mahbub from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would go to work with large quantities of date stones. He was asked, ‘What are these, O abu al-Hassan?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘They are palm trees, by the will of Allah.’ He then planted them and not a single piece failed sprouting.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/19)

parts.text: الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ

text: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) يَعْمَلُ فِي أَرْضٍ لَهُ قَدِ اسْتَنْقَعَتْ قَدَمَاهُ فِي الْعَرَقِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ أَيْنَ الرِّجَالُ فَقَالَ يَا عَلِيُّ قَدْ عَمِلَ بِالْيَدِ مَنْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِنِّي فِي أَرْضِهِ وَ مِنْ أَبِي فَقُلْتُ لَهُ وَ مَنْ هُوَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ آبَائِي ( عليهم السلام ) كُلُّهُمْ كَانُوا قَدْ عَمِلُوا بِأَيْدِيهِمْ وَ هُوَ مِنْ عَمَلِ النَّبِيِّينَ وَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ وَ الْأَوْصِيَاءِ وَ الصَّالِحِينَ .

en.hubeali: Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al Jamourany, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from his father who said,

‘(The narrator says) I saw Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> working in a land of his<sup>asws</sup>. His<sup>asws</sup> feet were drenched in the sweat, so I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! Where are the men (workers)?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘O Ali! There have worked by their hands in their land, the ones who were better than I<sup>asws</sup> and my<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup>’. So I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Who are they?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> and Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>, and my<sup>asws</sup> forefathers<sup>asws</sup>, all of them<sup>asws</sup> had worked by their<sup>asws</sup> hands, and it is from the deeds of the Prophets<sup>as</sup>, and the Mursil Prophets<sup>as</sup>, and the successors<sup>as</sup>, and the righteous ones’.
en.sarwar: 10. Sahl ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Jamuraniy from al-Hassan ibn Ali ibn abu Hamzah from his father who has said the following: “I once saw abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, working in his land and his feet were drenched in perspiration. I asked, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, where are men (workers)?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘O Ali, people better than my father and I worked in their land.’ I then asked, ‘Who were they?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘They were the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, and ’Amir al- Mu’minin, and my ancestors, Alayhim al-Salam, all of them worked with their hands and it is of the practice of the prophets, messengers, the executors of the wills and the virtuous people.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/19)
text: أَتَيْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ إِذَا هُوَ فِي حَائِطٍ لَهُ بِيَدِهِ مِسْحَاةٌ وَ هُوَ يَفْتَحُ بِهَا الْمَاءَ وَ عَلَيْهِ قَمِيصٌ شِبْهَ الْكَرَابِيسِ كَأَنَّهُ مَخِيطٌ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ ضِيقِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Sinan, from Ismail Bin Jabir who said,

‘I went over to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> was by a wall of his<sup>asws</sup> and in his<sup>asws</sup> hand was a spade, and he<sup>asws</sup> was opening the water (way) with it, and upon him<sup>asws</sup> was a shirt of cotton threads, as if it was stitched upon him<sup>asws</sup> due to its tightness.
en.sarwar: 11. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Sinan from ‘Isma’il ibn Jabir who has said the following: “I once visited abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and found him inside his walls with a shovel in his hand. He was opening the waterway. His shirt was of a fabric made of thick cotton threads (karabis) which was of very small size for him.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/20)
text: أَعْطَى أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَبِي أَلْفاً وَ سَبْعَمِائَةِ دِينَارٍ فَقَالَ لَهُ اتَّجِرْ بِهَا ثُمَّ قَالَ أَمَا إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ لِي رَغْبَةٌ فِي رِبْحِهَا وَ إِنْ كَانَ الرِّبْحُ مَرْغُوباً فِيهِ وَ لَكِنِّي أَحْبَبْتُ أَنْ يَرَانِيَ اللَّهُ جَلَّ وَ عَزَّ مُتَعَرِّضاً لِفَوَائِدِهِ

قَالَ فَرَبِحْتُ لَهُ فِيهَا مِائَةَ دِينَارٍ ثُمَّ لَقِيتُهُ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ قَدْ رَبِحْتُ لَكَ فِيهَا مِائَةَ دِينَارٍ قَالَ فَفَرِحَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) بِذَلِكَ فَرَحاً شَدِيداً فَقَالَ لِي أَثْبِتْهَا فِي رَأْسِ مَالِي

قَالَ فَمَاتَ أَبِي وَ الْمَالُ عِنْدَهُ فَأَرْسَلَ إِلَيَّ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَكَتَبَ عَافَانَا اللَّهُ وَ إِيَّاكَ إِنَّ لِي عِنْدَ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ أَلْفاً وَ ثَمَانَمِائَةِ دِينَارٍ أَعْطَيْتُهُ يَتَّجِرُ بِهَا فَادْفَعْهَا إِلَى عُمَرَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ

قَالَ فَنَظَرْتُ فِي كِتَابِ أَبِي فَإِذاً فِيهِ لِأَبِي مُوسَى عِنْدِي أَلْفٌ وَ سَبْعُمِائَةِ دِينَارٍ وَ اتُّجِرَ لَهُ فِيهَا مِائَةُ دِينَارٍ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ وَ عُمَرُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ يَعْرِفَانِهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ali Bin Asbaat, from Muhammad Bin Uzafir, from his father who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> gave my father one thousand and seven hundred Dinars, so he<sup>asws</sup> said to him: ‘Trade with these’. Then he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no real interest for me<sup>asws</sup> in its profits, although the profits are desireable in it, but I<sup>asws</sup> it that Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Majestic and Mighty Sees me<sup>asws</sup> working for its benefits’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘So I made a gain of one hundred Dinars for him in it. Then I met up with him<sup>asws</sup>, so I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘I have made a gain for you in it, of one hundred Dinars’. So Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> was happy with that with an intense happiness, and he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘Corroborate it in the capital of my<sup>asws</sup> wealth’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘So my father dies and the wealth was with him. So Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> sent a message to me, writing: ‘May Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Grant us<sup>asws</sup> and you good health. There is for me<sup>asws</sup> with your father Muhammad, one thousand and eight hundred Dinars, I<sup>asws</sup> had given him to trade with. So hand it over to Umar Bin Yazeed’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘So I looked in the book of my father, so there was in it for Abu Musa<sup>asws</sup>, with me, a thousand and seven hundred Dinars, and I had profited for him<sup>asws</sup> in it by one hundred Dinars which Abdullah Bin Sinan and Umar Bin Yazeed both knew of it’.
en.sarwar: 12. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ali ibn Asbat from Muhammad ibn ‘Adhafir from his father who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once gave my father one thousand seven hundred dinars and told him to use them in business. He (the Imam) then said, ‘I am not interested in its profits, even though profits are desirable. I love, however, that Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, see me work to make profits.’ He (the narrator) has said, ‘I then made one hundred dinar profit for him. I met him and informed him about the one hundred dinar profits.’ He (the narrator) has said that abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, became very happy about it and told me to keep it with his capital. He (the narrator) has said that my father passed away and goods were with him. Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, then wrote to me, ‘May Allah grant to us and to you good health. There is my one thousand eight hundred dinar with abu Muhammad which I had given him to use in business. You must give this amount to ‘Umar ibn Yazid.’ He (the narrator) has said, that I then checked the record of my father and found that one thousand seven hundred dinars were due in favor of abu Musa, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and I had earned one hundred dinars profit, ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan and ‘Umar ibn Yazid knew him.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/20)
text: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ بِيَدِهِ مِسْحَاةٌ وَ عَلَيْهِ إِزَارٌ غَلِيظٌ يَعْمَلُ فِي حَائِطٍ لَهُ وَ الْعَرَقُ يَتَصَابُّ عَنْ ظَهْرِهِ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ أَعْطِنِي أَكْفِكَ فَقَالَ لِي إِنِّي أُحِبُّ أَنْ يَتَأَذَّى الرَّجُلُ بِحَرِّ الشَّمْسِ فِي طَلَبِ الْمَعِيشَةِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from his father, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Al Qasim Bin Suleyman, from Jameel Bin Salih, from Abu Amro Al Shaybani who said,

‘I saw Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, and in his<sup>asws</sup> hand was a shovel, and upon him<sup>asws</sup> was a coarse trouser, working in a wall of his<sup>asws</sup>, and the sweat was running from his<sup>asws</sup> back. So I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! Give it to me, I shall suffice for you<sup>asws</sup>’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> love it that the man should be hurt by the heat of the sun in seeking the livelihood’.
en.sarwar: 13. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn ‘Abd Allah from his father from al-Nadr ibn Suwayd from al-Qasim ibn Muhammad ibn Sulayman who has said that narrated to him Jamil ibn Salih from abu ‘Amr al-Shaybaniy who has said the following: “I once saw abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, with a shovel in his hand wearing a very thick dress working inside his walls and his back drenched in perspiration. I then asked, I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, allow me to help you.’ He said to me, ‘I Love that man endures hardships in the hot sun to make a living.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/21)
text: إِنَّ رَجُلًا أَتَى أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ إِنِّي لَا أُحْسِنُ أَنْ أَعْمَلَ عَمَلًا بِيَدِي وَ لَا أُحْسِنُ أَنْ أَتَّجِرَ وَ أَنَا مُحَارَفٌ مُحْتَاجٌ فَقَالَ اعْمَلْ فَاحْمِلْ عَلَى رَأْسِكَ وَ اسْتَغْنِ عَنِ النَّاسِ فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) قَدْ حَمَلَ حَجَراً عَلَى عَاتِقِهِ فَوَضَعَهُ فِي حَائِطٍ لَهُ مِنْ حِيطَانِهِ وَ إِنَّ الْحَجَرَ لَفِي مَكَانِهِ وَ لَا يُدْرَى كَمْ عُمْقُهُ إِلَّا أَنَّهُ ثَمَّ [بِمُعْجِزَتِهِ] .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from IBn Abu Umeyr, from Umar Bin Azina, from Zurara, that,

‘A man came over to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, so he said, ‘I am not good at doing manual work with my hands, and I am not good at trading, and I am deprived, needy’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Work, so carry upon your head, and be needless from the people, for Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> had carried rocks upon his<sup>saww</sup> shoulders, and he<sup>saww</sup> placed then in a wall from the walls of his<sup>saww</sup>, and the rock is (still) in its place, and it is not known how much its depth is, except that it is there (by a miracle)’.
en.sarwar: 14. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ‘Umar ibn ‘Udhaynah from Zurarah who has said the following: “Once a man came to abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and said, ‘I am not good in working with my hands and I am not good in doing business, thus I am deprived and needy.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘You must work and carry it on your head and set yourself free from asking people for help. The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, carried a stone on his shoulder to place it in a certain place of his walls. The stone is still in its place, and it is not known how large it is, but it is there [by his miracle]”’

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/21)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ إِنِّي لَأَعْمَلُ فِي بَعْضِ ضِيَاعِي حَتَّى أَعْرَقَ وَ إِنَّ لِي مَنْ يَكْفِينِي لِيَعْلَمَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَنِّي أَطْلُبُ الرِّزْقَ الْحَلَالَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Husayn Bin saeed, from Al Qasim Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> tend to work in a site of mine<sup>asws</sup> until I<sup>asws</sup> sweat, altthough there are for me<sup>asws</sup> ones who can suffice for me<sup>asws</sup>, in order for Allah<sup>azwj</sup> to Know that I<sup>asws</sup> seek the Permissible livelihood’.
en.sarwar: 15. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from al-Qasim ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn abu Hamzah from abu Basir from who has said the following: “I once heard abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, T work in a certain site of my work to perspire, even though there are people who can do all the work instead of me but I work so Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, finds me striving to find lawful sustenance.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/21)
text: دَفَعَ إِلَيَّ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) سَبْعَمِائَةِ دِينَارٍ وَ قَالَ يَا عُذَافِرُ اصْرِفْهَا فِي شَيْ‏ءٍ أَمَّا عَلَى ذَاكَ مَا بِي شَرَهٌ وَ لَكِنْ أَحْبَبْتُ أَنْ يَرَانِيَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مُتَعَرِّضاً لِفَوَائِدِهِ
قَالَ عُذَافِرٌ فَرَبِحْتُ فِيهَا مِائَةَ دِينَارٍ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ فِي الطَّوَافِ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَدْ رَزَقَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِيهَا مِائَةَ دِينَارٍ فَقَالَ أَثْبِتْهَا فِي رَأْسِ مَالِي .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Muhammad Bin Uzafir, from his father who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> handed over seven hundred Dinars to me and said, ‘O Uzafir! Spend it in something, but upon that is not greed for me<sup>asws</sup>, but I<sup>asws</sup> love it that Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would See me<sup>asws</sup> striving for its benefits’.

Uzafir said, ‘So I made a gain in it of one hundred Dinars, so I said to him<sup>asws</sup> during the ‘الطَّوَافِ’ circumambulation, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic has Graced one hundred Dinars in it’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Corroborate (include) it in the capital of my<sup>asws</sup> wealth’.
en.sarwar: 16. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from Muhammad ibn ‘Isma’il from Muhammad ibn ‘Adha’fir from his father who has said the following: “Once abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, gave me seven hundred dinars and said, ‘O ‘Adha’fir you can spend it; I do not have any desire for it but I loved that Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, finds me working for its profits.’ ‘Adha’fir has said, ‘I made one hundred dinar profit with it. During al- Tawaf I said to him, “I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, has granted one hundred dinar profit.’” He (the Imam) said, ‘Add it to my capital money.’”
